mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS monthly change on 20-day forward returns below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative hit rate > 60% in top tertile of RSAFS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 6.5%, it indicates significant labor market slack, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In months with UNRATE > 6.5%, median 20-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10) or risk-adjusted return does not improve over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high CPI inflation (above 3% YoY) and low Fed funds rate (below 2%) create negative real interest rates that signal future monetary tightening or stagflation risk, depressing equity valuations over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS < 2%, 20-day forward returns are not statistically different from unconditional mean (permutation test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery regimes, industrial production (INDPRO) annual growth exceeding 3% confirms strong manufacturing momentum, which drives corporate investment and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: In post-recession quarters with INDPRO YoY > 3%, 60-day forward returns are not positive on average (one-sided test p>0.05) or rank IC < 0.02 over all such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.1%, it signals the central bank has reached the zero lower bound and is committed to aggressive easing, boosting equity prices through lower discount rates over the following week.
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days following FEDFUNDS < 0.1%, average return is not positive (t-test p>0.05) or subsequent volatility does not decrease.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strength in the residential construction sector, which boosts economic growth and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Monthly HOUST changes have a rank IC below 0.03 on 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above 0.5% indicates the economy is expanding, which supports corporate earnings and stock prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In quarters with GDP growth > 0.5%, subsequent 60-day average return is not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals imminent labor market weakness, triggering negative investor sentiment and selling pressure that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: In weeks following a PAYEMS decline, median 5-day forward return is not negative (signed-rank test p>0.05) or hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.
